title: lomatium triternatum f. lancifolium
intro: Lomatium triternatum f. lancifolium, also known as Fernleaf Biscuitroot, is a subspecies of the Lomatium triternatum plant, a flowering plant native to the western United States and Canada. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, its medicinal properties attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Lomatium triternatum f. lancifolium include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and pneumonia, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with the common cold. Lomatium triternatum f. lancifolium can be prepared for herbal use through infusion, tincture, or salve, with the dried roots or leaves typically being used in these preparations.
